title: Celegia
content:

'''Celegia''' was an [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of raging [[Ocean/Legends|seas]]. Its [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] was rich in the [[Poison/Legends|toxic]] [[cyanogen]] [[Gas/Legends|gas]], and it was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Celegian/Legends|Celegians]]. Due to [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]] being [[Death/Legends|lethal]] to the Celegians, they had to use special [[Celegian life support chamber|life support chambers]] when traveling off-world. Celegia had become a part of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] by approximately [[17,000 BBY]] and was a member of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]].

==Description==
Celegia was a [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="UAA" /> located in the [[Celegia system/Legends|Celegia system]], a part of the [[Noori sector/Legends|Noori sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="EAOC" /> By approximately [[17,000 BBY]], Celegia was connected via [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] to the [[Belasco system/Legends|Belasco system]] as well as the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim's]] [[Virujansi system/Legends|Virujansi system]].<ref name="EGTW" /> Those [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] routes persisted until the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] of [[3996 BBY]] but had ceased to exist by the onset of the [[Mandalorian Wars]] in [[3976 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" />

A world of raging [[Water/Legends|seas]], Celegia had both land and at least one [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]] on its surface. Its [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] had abundant amounts of [[cyanogen]], a [[Gas/Legends|gas]] that was [[Poison/Legends|toxic]] to most [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] but which the native [[Celegian/Legends|Celegians]] breathed.<ref name="UAA" />

==History==
[[File:CelegiaAlsakanConflicts-EGTW.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Celegia was a part of the Exploitation Region around 17,000 BBY.]]
[[Realspace/Legends|Space]] surrounding Celegia was [[Scout/Legends|explored]] between [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000 BBY]] and [[15,000 BBY/Legends|15,000 BBY]],<ref name="TEA" /> and the world was a part of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] by 17,000 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]]. Around that [[Time/Legends|time]], which saw the [[First Alsakan Conflict|beginning]] of a [[Alsakan Conflicts/Legends|series]] of [[Politics/Legends|conflicts]] between the planet [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]]; the Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world, [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]; and their respective allies, Celegia was situated in the [[Exploitation Region]], which was a territory actively controlled by the [[Grand Companies]]—Coruscant-affiliated [[Merchant/Legends|trading]] conglomerates based along the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] hyperlane known as [[the Spin]]. In addition, the hyperspace route connecting Celegia to the Belasco [[Star system/Legends|system]] was under the control of Coruscant. The hyperlane leading to the Virujansi system, however, was among the routes that were either neutral or contested during the conflicts.<ref name="EGTW" />

During the first campaigns of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] of [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]–[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the Republic's [[Fourth Outer Rim Army|Fourth Sector Army]] was charged with engaging the [[Confederacy military/Legends|military forces]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] in Celegia's vicinity. The world had become a part of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] by [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]].<ref name="EGTW" /> As part of their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], at some point by [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] gained control over the Celegia system.<ref name="TEA" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Celegian/Legends}}
[[File:Celegian_UAA.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Celegia was the homeworld of the Celegians.]]
Celegia was the ancient [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the sentient, [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] scyphozoans known as the Celegians. Evolved from ocean-dwelling [[invertebrate]]s that eventually adapted to living on land, the Celegians used for locomotion a natural form of levitation that resembled an organic [[Repulsorlift generator/Legends|repulsorlift generator]].<ref name="UAA" /> Around [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the population of the Celegia system numbered between ten and one hundred million.<ref name="TEA" />

[[Oxygen/Legends|Oxygen]],<ref name="UAA" /> which was commonly encountered off-world,<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|115|Celegian}}</ref> was [[Death/Legends|lethal]] to Celegians, necessitating the use of special [[Celegian life support chamber|life support chambers]] when members of the species were away from their homeworld.<ref name="UAA" /> Despite that limitation, Celegians eventually established a [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Tramanos]], which also had cyanogen in its atmosphere.<ref name="Gazetter">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-the-knight-errant-gazetter|text=Essential Atlas Extra: The Knight Errant Gazetteer|int=The Knight Errant Gazetteer}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Celegia was first indirectly mentioned in the [[2002]] ''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'', which was published by [[Wizards of the Coast]] for use with the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="POTJ">''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''</ref> The world was first named in the [[2003]] ''Star Wars Roleplaying Game'' sourcebook ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''.<ref name="UAA" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Celegia system, and therefore Celegia itself, in grid square O-10.<ref name="TEA" />
